metadata.disclaimer: Prices are approximate retail as of the update date. No endorsement of specific brands is implied — these are widely available, proven products.

budget.label: Budget Setup ($150-250)
budget.rod: Ugly Stik Tiger Lite, 5'6" medium-heavy conventional ($60)
budget.terminal: 16/0 Eagle Claw circle hooks (pack of 25), 16-24 oz cannonball sinkers, barrel swivels ($30)
budget.notes: Perfectly functional setup that will land halibut to 100+ pounds. Upgrade the reel first when ready.

midrange.label: Mid-Range Setup ($350-550)
midrange.terminal: 16/0 Owner circle hooks, 24-32 oz cannonball sinkers, Sampo swivels ($40)
midrange.notes: The sweet spot of performance and price. This is the setup most serious private boaters run.

premium.label: Premium Setup ($700-1200)
premium.rod: Shimano Talula TESC-66XH or custom wrapped rod ($200-400)
premium.line: 80 lb Hollow-Ace braid with wind-on leaders ($50)
premium.terminal: Mustad Demon circle hooks, custom snelled leaders, titanium spreader bars ($80)
premium.notes: Charter-grade equipment. The two-speed reel is essential for cranking big fish from deep water.

budget.label: Budget Setup ($150-200)
budget.reel: Daiwa Sealine SG-27LC levelwind with line counter ($80)
budget.terminal: Pro-Troll flasher, hoochies, 30 lb fluorocarbon leader ($30)
budget.notes: Monofilament has stretch which can actually help with downrigger fishing. Line counter is essential.

midrange.label: Mid-Range Setup ($300-500)
midrange.reel: Shimano Tekota 300LC levelwind with line counter ($180)
midrange.terminal: Pro-Troll E-Chip flashers, hoochies, Coyote spoons, 40 lb fluorocarbon leader ($50)
midrange.notes: The standard Alaska charter boat setup. Reliable, proven, and versatile for both kings and silvers.

premium.label: Premium Setup ($600-900)
premium.line: 50 lb braid with topshot of 30 lb fluorocarbon ($40)
premium.terminal: Pro-Troll E-Chip flashers, whole herring rigs, custom tied leaders ($80)
premium.notes: Glass rods have a softer action that keeps hooks pinned during the fight. Preferred by many guides.

shoreSpin.label: Shore Spin Fishing Setup ($100-180)
shoreSpin.rod: 8' medium action spinning rod — St. Croix Premier or Shimano Convergence ($50-80)
shoreSpin.reel: Size 3000 spinning reel — Shimano Sienna or Daiwa Regal ($40)
shoreSpin.lures: Pixie spoons in pink and orange, Vibrax spinners size 3-4, assorted colors ($30-50)
shoreSpin.notes: All you need for productive shore fishing for silvers, pinks, and Dollies.

boatTroll.label: Boat Trolling Setup (uses king salmon gear with modifications)
boatTroll.modifications: Shorter hoochie leaders (24-30 inches), pink/chartreuse colors, faster trolling speed (2.5-3.5 mph)
boatTroll.notes: Silver trolling uses the same gear as king trolling with different lure selections and faster speeds.

flyFishing.label: Fly Fishing Setup ($200-400)
flyFishing.rod: 8 weight, 9 foot fly rod — Orvis Clearwater or Echo Boost ($150-200)
flyFishing.reel: Large arbor reel with disc drag — Lamson Liquid or Ross Flyrise ($80-100)
flyFishing.line: Weight-forward floating line with 10' fast-sink tip ($50)
flyFishing.flies: Clouser Minnows, Dolly Llamas, Pollywogs in pink, white, chartreuse ($2-5 each, get 10-15)
flyFishing.notes: An 8-weight handles silvers well. Go to 9 weight if you might encounter kings.

budget.label: Budget Setup ($100-150)
budget.reel: Size 4000 spinning reel with 20 lb braid ($50)
budget.terminal: 4-8 oz leadhead jigs, 6" curly-tail grubs in white and chartreuse ($20)
budget.notes: Works for rockfish to 200 feet. Beyond that, a conventional reel is better for cranking weight.

midrange.label: Mid-Range Setup ($250-400)
midrange.terminal: Butterfly jigs (4-10 oz), swimbaits, dropper loop rigs ($50)
midrange.notes: Handles rockfish and lingcod at any depth. Also usable as a backup halibut rod.

starter.label: Complete Starter Package ($200-350)
starter.description: Everything needed for shore fishing silvers, pinks, and Dollies plus basic boat bottom fishing
items: 15 lb braid for spinning, 65 lb braid for conventional ($45)
Fluorocarbon leader material: 12 lb and 80 lb ($20)
Assorted spoons, spinners, and circle hooks ($40)

weekend.label: Weekend Angler Package ($600-1000)
weekend.description: Two complete setups covering all major Alaska species from boat and shore
items: Downrigger rod and levelwind reel with line counter ($200)
Complete terminal tackle box with hooks, weights, flashers, hoochies, spoons ($150)
Landing net, gaff, fish club, fillet knife ($80)

serious.label: Serious Angler Package ($1500-3000)
serious.description: Four complete setups plus boat equipment for dedicated Alaska fishing
items: Fly fishing setup for trout and salmon ($250-400)
Safety equipment: PFDs, VHF radio, first aid kit ($200)
Fish processing: cooler, bags, bleeding bucket, fillet knives ($100)

spring.notes: Early season weather is unpredictable. Focus on saltwater trolling near Homer and Seward.

essentialGear: Sockeye flossing rig (long leader, coho fly)
summer.notes: Peak season. Everything is happening. Book charters and accommodations well in advance.

lateSummer.notes: Silver salmon dominate. The Homer Jackpot Halibut Derby runs through Labor Day.

essentialGear: Bead fishing setup (float rod, beads, fluorocarbon)
Fly fishing setup for streamers and flesh flies
fall.notes: The quiet season. Fewer anglers, beautiful fall colors, excellent trout fishing on the Kenai.
